error_signature: %error-blkloopinit: <file>:<n>: unsupported: delayed assignment to array inside for loops (non-delayed is ok - see docs) <n> | s[i] <= '<n>; | ^ ... for error description see https://verilator.org/warn/blkloopinit?v=<n>.<n> %error: exiting due to <n> error(s) ... see the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance
raw_error_example:
%Error-BLKLOOPINIT: output/fir_bandpass_xposed_rand.sv:123:18: Unsupported: Delayed assignment to array inside for loops (non-delayed is ok - see docs)
  123 |                 s[i] <= '0;
      |                  ^
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/BLKLOOPINIT?v=5.020
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
root_cause: Verilator does not support non-blocking assignments to array elements inside for loops in sequential logic due to limitations in its static analysis.
fix_rule: Replace non-blocking assignments with blocking assignments when initializing arrays inside for loops in sequential blocks. Use a separate always block with blocking assignments for initialization.
